user_outcome: An operator can inspect historical records read-only with committed version evidence, while a live-operation preflight fails before side effects unless the exact release protocol marker matches the binary and every participating authority ref.
rationale: This slice owns C-03 and separates truthful archive inspection from current mutation authority. Evidence is derived from committed objects, never record shape or directory naming. Default output contains metadata and validation results only; exact record bytes appear solely under an explicit raw archive option and are never logged. The CLI is keyboard/text accessible, no network is used, and first-parent history work is linear in commits inspected with object lookups cached per invocation.
in_scope: Embed and strictly decode release-protocol-authority-v1 with record_version 1, matching release ID, protocol name/version, upstream SHA, sha256 digest, VERSION blob OID, native-or-migrated origin, and planning-or-current authority; reject duplicate or unknown keys and require a release-confined migration receipt path for migrated origin.

Classify every registered CLI spelling/operation and every MCP tool with exactly one of the six section-4 policies: protocol_independent, read_only_inspection, planning_validation, activation, current, or retired; exhaustively cover all live names, aliases and mixed subcommand routes, reject an unclassified registration, and run the required preflight before its handler can write, launch, dispatch, merge, commit, or push.

Implement RequireLive to read one committed protocol.json blob from the authority ref, require byte identity on every explicitly participating ref, resolve its vendored VERSION blob, and compare all pin fields to the running binary before the caller may perform side effects.

Implement archive inspection for one requested committed record: resolve the record blob, walk first-parent history newest-to-oldest across the current uninterrupted equal-blob suffix, select that suffix's oldest commit, read exact internal/adopt/baton/VERSION from that tree, and return the record blob plus evidence commit, version path/blob, and resolved pin metadata.

Add sworn baton protocol check --release <name> --authority-ref <ref> [--participant-ref <ref>...] and sworn baton protocol inspect --release <name> --record <path> --ref <ref> [--raw] as read-only public policy/provenance surfaces with JSON output and fail-closed exit codes.

Treat missing, uncommitted-only, deleted, divergent, malformed, unsupported, shallow-history, missing-object, conflicting-version, or binary-mismatched evidence as non-authoritative; raw mode may emit exact committed record bytes but may not claim validation.
Prove archive inspection leaves worktree bytes, index, refs, board files, and status files unchanged and never invokes any current record writer or lazy board migration.
out_of_scope: Bulk rewriting historical records or synthesizing v0.15 reports is forbidden; pristine active-plan migration belongs to S17-engine-replan-migration.
Wiring RequireLive into maintainability, loop, merge, release, ship, and re-plan success paths belongs to C-03's declared consumer slices S13 through S18.
Network retrieval of missing Git objects or protocol content is prohibited; shallow or incomplete evidence fails closed.
Changing the embedded C-01 pin or vendor content belongs to S02-v015-parity-and-installs.

effort_complexity.rationale: The slice defines a new strict authority record, cross-ref byte identity, first-parent historical provenance, read-only public commands, and conservative failure semantics.

risks.description: Record shape, folder date, or current binary version is mistaken for historical protocol evidence.
risks.mitigation: Accept only the exact committed record blob, first-parent introduction commit, and VERSION blob from that same tree; structural heuristics have no authority path.

risks.description: A live marker is valid on one ref but stale or dirty-only on another participant.
risks.mitigation: Resolve committed blobs independently on every named ref, compare exact bytes and embedded pin fields, and test dirty worktree overlays plus divergent refs before any side-effect callback.

risks.description: Archive inspection mutates the repository through a reused current reader or lazy migration.
risks.mitigation: Use object-database-only reads, snapshot status/index/refs/worktree before inspection, and assert byte identity after success and every failure case.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the public baton protocol check and inspect commands run against a temporary repository containing live and historical records THE SYSTEM SHALL enforce C-03, return committed pin/provenance metadata, pair authority with exit code, and leave the worktree, index, refs, board, and status byte-identical (N-01, N-02, N-04, N-09, N-10, N-11).

WHEN RequireLive receives an authority ref and derived participating refs whose committed protocol.json bytes and VERSION blob equal C-01 and C-03 THE SYSTEM SHALL return one validated immutable authority object before invoking the supplied operation callback; all 39 live CLI spellings plus mixed journeys/ledger routes and all 26 live MCP tools SHALL have exactly one exhaustive operation-level classification from planning/normative-clarifications.md section 4 before handlers execute, and an unclassified or unknown spelling SHALL fail registration/dispatch, with unknown CLI exiting 64.

IF any live marker is missing, dirty-only, deleted, divergent, malformed, release-mismatched, unsupported, or binary-mismatched THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return non-authoritative before the callback, commit, write, dispatch, or push counter changes.

IF planning validation lacks exact planning-or-current C-03 authority, current policy lacks exact current C-03 authority, or activation does not match exactly one origin-specific edge—C-13 native cutover, C-12 pristine migration, or C-12 migrated activation—THEN dispatch SHALL exit non-zero before invoking its handler; protocol-independent and archive-inspection operations SHALL receive no mutation authority, and no flag, alias, or delta content MAY reclassify an operation.

WHEN archive inspection requests a committed record THE SYSTEM SHALL walk first-parent history newest-to-oldest while the path resolves to the current exact blob, return the oldest commit in that uninterrupted equal-blob suffix, and return exact internal/adopt/baton/VERSION path/blob/pin from that tree so deletion and later reintroduction select the most recent introduction; IF the boundary cannot be proven, history is shallow, objects are missing, versions conflict, or resolution is otherwise impossible THEN validation SHALL fail and raw mode SHALL never claim authority.
